title: cassine bupleuroides
intro: Cassine bupleuroides, also known as Cassine or Wild Privet, is a small tree or shrub native to Africa, particularly in tropical regions. Cassine bupleuroides has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as the relief of pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, Cassine bupleuroides is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
